families.literature_identity: Matched-market / greedy geo market pairing for geo experiments
canonical_reference_or_reference_family: Google geo experiments / matched markets (industry practice; see Brodersen et al. 2015 CausalImpact context for geo lift)
Matched-pair and market-selection design literature (experimental design texts)
families.canonical_estimand: Valid randomization of markets to treatment/control with balance on pre-treatment outcomes — not a causal ATT by itself
families.required_data_geometry: Unit-level panel (markets/DMAs); assignment dict control + test_*
families.supported_treatment_structure: Single or multi test cells via n_test_grps
assumptions: Pre-treatment outcomes used for matching only (no post-treatment leakage in design)
recommended_or_valid_inference_methods: Design stage only; analysis typically paired TBR or SCM family (design–analysis alignment required)
diagnostics_expected_by_literature: Treatment fraction on KPI mass vs unit count if product uses share targets
known_failure_modes: Look-ahead if matching uses post-treatment periods in wide panel (INV-D1-001)
Donor pool for analysis ≠ markets used in design matching
Too few controls for intended readout
implementation_questions_to_resolve: Confirm pre_treatment_period always passed from GeoExperimentDesign/geo_runner
Document KPI-mass vs unit-count treatment probability semantics
statistical_validation_needed: Balance metrics vs analysis estimand under 001e-style worlds
Sensitivity when post periods present in caller wide data
families.next_layer_3_action: GeoExperimentDesign period slicing audit; MAT-001 reconciliation with SCM donors

families.literature_identity: Rerandomization / restricted randomization to improve covariate balance
canonical_reference_or_reference_family: Morgan & Rubin (2012) — Rerandomization to improve balance
Li & Ding (2017) — rerandomization inference (when inference claimed)
families.canonical_estimand: Assignment distribution subject to balance criterion — not ATT
families.required_data_geometry: Same as wrapped base randomizer (typically unit assignment dict)
families.supported_treatment_structure: Via base design (tier-1 randomizers or greedy base)
assumptions: Balance metric reflects scientifically relevant pre-treatment differences
Acceptance rule does not induce selection bias beyond stated design
recommended_or_valid_inference_methods: Randomization-based inference when design is pure randomization; rerandomization p-values if formally implemented (often not in product path)
known_failure_modes: Different production path than bare greedy (OC sensitivity)
repo_implementation_variants: rerandomization_wrapper row — not separate registry name
implementation_questions_to_resolve: Map imbalance metric to literature balance definitions
Compare rerandomization_wrapper vs bare greedy_match_markets in Layer 4
statistical_validation_needed: D5-POW-001e rerandomization vs bare tier-1 sensitivity
families.next_layer_3_action: Document wrapper dispatch in GeoExperimentDesign; D1 DES-010a checks

families.literature_identity: Complete / balanced / stratified / thinning randomization for geo units
canonical_reference_or_reference_family: Fisher–Yates / stratified experiment design (standard texts)
Kerner thinning designs where thinningdesign maps to industry thinning practice
families.canonical_estimand: Probabilistic assignment to arms — not ATT
families.required_data_geometry: Unit panel; geo_run assignment dict
recommended_or_valid_inference_methods: Randomization inference for arms; often paired with SCM+JK null-monitor in repo OC (compatibility not automatic)
known_failure_modes: Design–readout mismatch when analysis assumes matched markets
implementation_questions_to_resolve: Thinning kernel parameters vs literature thinning
statistical_validation_needed: D5-POW-001e tier-1 arms with SCM+JK reference path
families.next_layer_3_action: Design inventory cross-check; design→estimator bridge for each mode

families.literature_identity: Supergeo / cluster-based geo pair construction (MILP-style geometry)
canonical_reference_or_reference_family: Industry supergeo / cluster market pairing (implementation-specific)
Cluster-randomized experiment literature where clusters are markets
families.canonical_estimand: Cluster-level assignment or pair structure — estimand depends on bridge
families.required_data_geometry: Supergeo pair/cluster output — not flat unit dict for SCM+JK without bridge
recommended_or_valid_inference_methods: Not SCM+UnitJackKnife on flat unit panel without geometry bridge
implementation_questions_to_resolve: Define estimand bridge to unit-panel estimators
Document MILP objective vs literature cluster designs
statistical_validation_needed: D5-DES-SUPERGEO-001 replay; blocked 001e combos until bridge
families.next_layer_3_action: F-GEO-003 adapter design; geometry bridge ADR

families.literature_identity: Trimmed matching / paired markets with distance trim (Tp/Te)
canonical_reference_or_reference_family: Trimmed matching / paired comparison designs (observational matching literature)
Geo trimmed-match product specs (implementation-specific)
families.canonical_estimand: ATT on trimmed population of pairs — requires explicit bridge
families.required_data_geometry: Tp/Te pair population — not standard flat SCM panel
assumptions: Trim rule preserves target population definition
recommended_or_valid_inference_methods: Pair-level analysis — not flat SCM+JK without bridge

canonical_reference_or_reference_family: Blocked / matched-pair experimental designs (standard texts)
families.canonical_estimand: Assignment — product-specific
families.required_data_geometry: Legacy assignment dict; not geo-run
assumptions: Caller supplies valid panel and blocking factors
recommended_or_valid_inference_methods: Not wired to production geo orchestration
known_failure_modes: Not on geo-run path; stale API risk

families.literature_identity: Synthetic control method (convex combination of donors)
canonical_reference_or_reference_family: Abadie & Gardeazabal (2003) — economic costs of conflict
Abadie, Diamond, Hainmueller (2015) — comparative politics SCM
Abadie (2021) — Using synthetic controls: https://doi.org/10.1257/jel.20191450
families.canonical_estimand: ATT or treated-unit gap vs weighted donor counterfactual (paper-specific; often unit-level treatment effect on treated)
families.required_data_geometry: Donor pool + treated unit(s); panel with explicit pre/post
families.supported_treatment_structure: Single or multiple treated units (estimand must be stated)
assumptions: Donors not affected by treatment (no spillover into donors)
Convex weights non-negative summing to one (standard SCM)
No extrapolation beyond convex hull without diagnostics
recommended_or_valid_inference_methods: Permutation / placebo / jackknife variants per Abadie et al. — not all map to classical i.i.d. CI
Repo: UnitJackKnife, Placebo, JKP, Kfold, BRB, Conformal (compatibility varies)
implementation_questions_to_resolve: Alias SCM → which class in production
statistical_validation_needed: SCM+JK null FPR batteries (001e, ASCM-002 context)

families.literature_identity: Augmented synthetic control (ridge-augmented bias correction + SCM leg)
canonical_reference_or_reference_family: Ben-Michael, Feller, Rothstein (2021) — The augmented synthetic control method: https://arxiv.org/abs/1811.08779
families.canonical_estimand: Augmented ATT-style gap on treated units (bias-corrected SCM)
families.required_data_geometry: Unit panel; per-cell fits for multi-cell
families.supported_treatment_structure: Single cell primary; multi-cell per-cell only (pooling not literature-default)
assumptions: Ridge augmentation role is bias correction not arbitrary ML
recommended_or_valid_inference_methods: Literature focuses on point + specialized inference; repo: point, Kfold, Conformal — JK/Placebo compatibility limited
known_failure_modes: SCM leg mismatch vs outcome leg (G4/G7/G8)
Pooling across cells invalid for causal estimand
families.next_layer_3_action: AugSynthCVXPY implementation validation; ADR pooling semantics only

families.literature_identity: Time-based regression / geo lift (Google TBR-style aggregate comparison)
canonical_reference_or_reference_family: Google Time-Based Regression geo experiment materials (industry)
Brodersen et al. (2015) CausalImpact / structural comparison context for geo series
families.canonical_estimand: Cumulative or relative lift on aggregate treated vs control series (product: relative_att_post family)
families.required_data_geometry: Strict 1 treated + 1 control aggregate series (TBR class)
families.supported_treatment_structure: Single treated row + single control row only
assumptions: Design aligns markets to aggregate series
Variance model adequate for inference paired later
recommended_or_valid_inference_methods: point, JK, JKP, Kfold in catalog — aggregate geometry only
implementation_questions_to_resolve: Cumulative vs relative estimand in code vs INV-003
families.next_layer_3_action: TBR aggregate fidelity + estimand registry mapping

canonical_reference_or_reference_family: Doudchenko & Imbens (2017) — balancing with synthetic controls
TBR/geo ridge lift product path (implementation extension)
families.canonical_estimand: Regularized counterfactual / lift (often unit-level; agg2 path for power)
families.required_data_geometry: Unit panel or 2-row aggregate (agg2) depending on call path
assumptions: Negative weights only if explicitly allowed
Pooled pivot semantics for JK/JKP understood
recommended_or_valid_inference_methods: Kfold, BRB, Conformal, TS-Kfold, JK, JKP per catalog — calibration required for JK/JKP
known_failure_modes: PowerAnalysis agg2 vs unit readout mismatch

canonical_reference_or_reference_family: Angrist & Pischke (2009) — Mostly Harmless Econometrics (DID chapter)
Callaway & Sant'Anna (2021) — staggered DID when timing heterogeneous (if claimed)
families.canonical_estimand: ATT or cumulative ATT depending on specification
families.required_data_geometry: Panel with treated and control units over time
families.supported_treatment_structure: Single or multiple treated units per implementation
assumptions: Staggered timing handled if multi-period adoption claimed
recommended_or_valid_inference_methods: Cluster/bootstrap CIs in literature
Repo: native bootstrap embedded in DID — not registry BlockResidualBootstrap
known_failure_modes: TWFE bias under staggered adoption if misapplied

canonical_reference_or_reference_family: Arkhangelsky, Athey, Hirshberg, Imbens, Wager (2021): https://doi.org/10.1093/restud/rdz044
families.canonical_estimand: ATT with unit and time weights
families.required_data_geometry: Panel with treatment timing
families.supported_treatment_structure: Staggered adoption possible in literature — repo research_only
recommended_or_valid_inference_methods: SDID bootstrap per paper — repo point only
known_failure_modes: Skipped in validation runner; fidelity unreviewed
implementation_questions_to_resolve: Map repo SDID to paper algorithm
statistical_validation_needed: Research charter OC before any product use

families.literature_identity: Bayesian structural time series / horseshoe regression (research)

families.canonical_estimand: Posterior counterfactual lift
families.required_data_geometry: Aggregate or unit series with covariates
recommended_or_valid_inference_methods: Bayesian credible intervals — registry Bayesian handler not full MCMC
known_failure_modes: Registry Bayesian on TBRRidge ≠ BayesianTBR NUTS (INV-015)
